actions: Read spec.md — RQ9 definition + scope constraints: PRAT closed-source (line 48: 'The underlying PRAT algorithm is closed-source'), SaaS endpoint exclusion (line 125: 'Calling the live xanther.ai SaaS endpoint. Research is offline against the public docs'), no reverse-engineering (line 126: 'Reverse-engineering proprietary PRAT internals from network traffic, decompilation, or any non-public source')

Read iteration-005.md — RQ5 PRAT DEFER verdict (F-029: 'Full PRAT pipeline: DEFER — closed-source; we cannot ADOPT the complete pipeline'), cascade hybrid reference (F-032: 'cascade hybrid variant +10.8pp suggests XCE supports a multi-pass strategy where context is fetched in stages')

Read iteration-006.md — RQ6 static ALWAYS steering gap: F-033 ('All 6 steering files converge: Always use X before reading files' — external/steering/CLAUDE.md:5, kiro.md:4, opencode-prompt.txt:1), F-034 gap analysis ('use' vs 'Always use'), F-037 ADAPT verdict ('XCE's static steering pattern cannot be ADOPTED directly because: (1) Architectural difference — XCE steering is static... Our advisor is dynamic... (2) Tool difference — XCE hard-codes the target tool')

Read iteration-007.md — RQ7 SWE-bench + mini-swe-agent closed-source: F-039 ('XCE uses SWE-bench Verified + mini-swe-agent... reproduces XCE's exact setup would require reverse-engineering their agent configuration, which is closed-source'), F-042 DEFER verdict

Read external/README.md:29,74 — PRAT name-only reference line 29 ('Powered by the PRAT algorithm'), SaaS endpoint URL line 74 ('url: https://mcp.xanther.ai/sse'), signup + API key lines 51-62 ('Sign up at xanther.ai/signup and generate an API key')

Read external/README.md:43,188,264-270 — cascade hybrid benchmark row line 43 ('Sonnet 4.0 + XCE (cascade hybrid): 76.8%'), token reduction claim line 188 ('~20% token reduction when the agent uses XCE proactively'), pricing tiers lines 264-270 (Free $0 / Starter $8/mo / Pro $15/mo / Unlimited $20/mo)
Read external/README.md:113,130,144,158,171,180 — 6 IDE steering variants ALL commanding 'Call xce_get_context first on any task' with unconditional 'Always use X before reading files' phrasing
Read external/README.md:229-244 — 'How It Works' section: index → connect → query → context pipeline, all via SaaS endpoint with Graph Store on server side
Read external/README.md:37-45 — SWE-bench Verified benchmark table using mini-swe-agent, Sonnet 4.0 + XCE 73.4%, cascade hybrid 76.8%

label: SKIP-1: Closed-source PRAT algorithm internals — PRAT mentioned by name only at external/README.md:29 ('Powered by the PRAT algorithm'). Algorithm implementation is closed-source SaaS running on xanther.ai. Spec.md:48 confirms 'The underlying PRAT algorithm is closed-source.' Spec.md:58 constrains: 'Research must reverse-engineer architectural intent from public docs + benchmark numbers + tool API shape, not from source.' RQ5 F-029 classified full pipeline as DEFER — 'closed-source; we cannot ADOPT the complete pipeline.' Individual stages (Persistent, Recursive, Abstract, Tree) were extracted via public surface analysis only.

evidence.quote: Powered by the PRAT (Persistent Recursive Abstract Tree) algorithm
The underlying PRAT (Persistent Recursive Abstract Tree) algorithm is closed-source
The PRAT algorithm is NOT visible. Research must reverse-engineer architectural intent from public docs + benchmark numbers + tool API shape, not from source

verdict: SKIP — closed-source internals; reverse-engineering only from public surface, never from source

label: SKIP-2: SaaS hosting model — XCE is a hosted MCP server at 'https://mcp.xanther.ai/sse' (external/README.md:74,88,91) requiring Bearer API-key Authorization header (README:76-78,91-93). Our system-spec-kit MCP server runs as a local Stdio transport with zero network dependencies — tool handlers, SQLite persistence, and advisor scoring are all in-process. The SaaS hosting model is architecturally incompatible: we cannot adopt a model where context resolution requires a remote network call to an external service. Spec.md:125 explicitly excludes 'Calling the live xanther.ai SaaS endpoint' from scope.

evidence.quote: url: https://mcp.xanther.ai/sse
headers: Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY
Calling the live xanther.ai SaaS endpoint. Research is offline against the public docs in external/ plus our local code

verdict: SKIP — SaaS hosted endpoint; we run local MCP with zero network deps

label: SKIP-3: Centralized xanther.ai dependency — XCE deployment requires (a) signup at xanther.ai/signup (README:53), (b) API key from app.xanther.ai (README:53), (c) npx xanther-cli init --api-key (README:60), (d) MCP config pointing to mcp.xanther.ai (README:74). All query resolution — search, architecture context, trace, impact analysis — routes through the remote SaaS endpoint (README:229-244: 'How It Works' diagram: Coding Agent ↔ XCE Server / PRAT Algorithm + Graph Store). Our system has zero external service dependencies: code-graph-db.ts is local SQLite, skill_advisor runs in-process, all tools are self-contained in the local spec-kit-memory MCP server.

evidence.quote: Get your API key — Sign up at xanther.ai/signup and generate an API key from the dashboard. Index your repository — npx xanther-cli init --api-key xce_your_key_here

How It Works — Xanther CLI or GitHub App indexes your repo into a knowledge graph → Add the MCP config → Your agent automatically calls XCE tools when it needs codebase context → XCE returns precise architectural context

verdict: SKIP — centralized external dependency; we run local self-contained MCP server

label: SKIP-4: SaaS pricing model — XCE uses tiered monthly pricing: Free ($0, 100 queries, 3 repos), Starter ($8/mo, 2K queries), Pro ($15/mo, 10K queries), Unlimited ($20/mo) (external/README.md:264-270). Our system is an open-source local tool with no query limits, no pricing tiers, no billing infrastructure. Adopting XCE's pricing-coupled query model would require instrumenting query-per-repo counters, rate-limit enforcement, and billing — all alien to our local open-source architecture. The pricing model is inseparable from XCE's SaaS business (it gates API-key issuance and endpoint access).

evidence.quote: Pricing: Free ($0, 100 queries, 3 repos), Starter ($8/mo, 2,000 queries, 5 repos), Pro ($15/mo, 10,000 queries, 20 repos), Unlimited ($20/mo, unlimited queries, unlimited repos)
hosted SaaS MCP server

verdict: SKIP — tiered SaaS pricing; we are open-source local tool with no query limits

label: SKIP-5: Static unconditional 'ALWAYS' steering — All 6 XCE steering files use hard-coded unconditional directives: 'Always use xanther-xce MCP tools before reading files' (external/steering/CLAUDE.md:5, kiro.md:4, opencode-prompt.txt:1). There is no confidence threshold, no dynamic skill selection, no 'don't invoke' case. Our skill_advisor is dynamic: brief only renders when confidence ≥ 0.8 AND uncertainty ≤ 0.35 (render.ts:124-133). Cannot adopt unconditional ALWAYS without surgeon-level scorer changes (out of scope per spec.md:129: 'Changes to skill_advisor/lib/scorer/ (scoring math). Render-layer changes only, and only as proposals'). RQ6 classified as ADAPT — strengthen directive to 'MUST invoke X FIRST' but preserve confidence gate.

evidence.quote: Always use xanther-xce MCP tools before reading files
Always use the xanther-xce MCP tools for codebase understanding before reading files directly
Always use xanther-xce MCP tools for codebase understanding before reading files
if (top.confidence >= THRESHOLD_CONFIDENCE && top.uncertainty <= THRESHOLD_UNCERTAINTY || top.passes_threshold === true) — threshold gate prevents unconditional firing

verdict: SKIP — unconditional ALWAYS steering; we have dynamic confidence-gated advisor (ADAPT not ADOPT)

label: SKIP-6: Hard-coded single-tool first call (xce_get_context) — All 6 XCE steering variants command 'Call xce_get_context as your FIRST step when starting any task' (README:113), 'Call xce_get_context first on any task' (README:130,144,158,171,180). ONE tool for ALL tasks, no skill routing. Our skill_advisor dynamically selects from 16 skills based on intent classification and confidence scoring (scorer/). Replacing dynamic routing with a hard-coded single tool would destroy the advisor's purpose — tasks like 'run deep research' would misroute to code_graph_context instead of deep-research skill. This is an architectural difference we cannot bridge without fundamentally redesigning our multi-skill advisor into a single-tool gate.

evidence.quote: Call xce_get_context as your FIRST step when starting any task. It combines search, architecture context, and tracing into one call
Call xce_get_context first on any task — it returns architecture, code locations, and relationships
Call xce_get_context as your first action on any task
confidence ≥ 0.8 threshold — dynamic single-skill selection from 16 candidates, not static single-tool

verdict: SKIP — hard-coded single-tool first call; we have dynamic 16-skill adaptive routing

label: SKIP-7: mini-swe-agent harness — XCE's SWE-bench evaluation uses 'mini-swe-agent' (external/README.md:37) — a specific closed-source agent scaffold with proprietary prompt templates, tool-calling loop, and evaluation integration. The agent scaffold is not documented in external/ beyond the one-line mention. RQ7 F-039: 'reproducing XCE's exact setup would require reverse-engineering their agent configuration, which is closed-source.' Our proposed local harness (RQ7 F-041) uses a custom labeled-task set + subprocess OpenCode dispatch instead.

evidence.quote: All results on SWE-bench Verified using mini-swe-agent
Calling the live xanther.ai SaaS endpoint. Research is offline against the public docs

mini-swe-agent is a specific agent scaffold with its own prompt templates and tool-calling loop. Reproducing XCE's exact setup would require reverse-engineering their agent configuration, which is closed-source

verdict: SKIP — closed-source mini-swe-agent; our eval uses custom labeled tasks + OpenCode dispatch

label: SKIP-8: SWE-bench Verified Docker infrastructure — XCE's benchmark runs on SWE-bench Verified, requiring Docker execution environments per task, the swe-bench-eval Python pipeline, 2,294 PRs across 12 Python repos, ~50GB+ Docker images (RQ7 F-039). Spec.md:127 explicitly excludes SWE-bench eval from scope. Not adoptable due to scale (50GB+ Docker, Python-only), language mismatch (our system is TypeScript), and scope exclusion. Our proposed local benchmark (RQ7 F-041) uses 12-20 refactoring tasks on our own TS codebase, zero Docker, zero external dataset dependency.

evidence.quote: All results on SWE-bench Verified using mini-swe-agent — Sonnet 4.0 baseline 66.0%, +XCE 73.4%, cascade hybrid 76.8%
SWE-bench evaluation against our stack. Benchmark methodology is documented as a follow-on RQ proposal, not run
SWE-bench Verified requires Docker execution environments, GitHub dataset downloads... ~50GB+ Docker images, per-task sandboxes. This is a heavyweight CI-scale infrastructure

verdict: SKIP — SWE-bench Docker infrastructure; our local eval uses zero Docker, 12-20 TS tasks

label: SKIP-9: Cascade hybrid multi-pass strategy — XCE benchmark row shows 'Sonnet 4.0 + XCE (cascade hybrid): 76.8%' (external/README.md:43), +3.4pp over standard XCE and +10.8pp over raw baseline. Mentioned only as a benchmark row — ZERO documentation about implementation: how passes are staged, how context cascades, or agent-loop mechanics. RQ5 F-032 notes inference only: 'suggests XCE supports a multi-pass strategy where context is fetched in stages.' Without documentation or source, we cannot determine implementation. Speculating violates spec.md REQ-006: 'Every claim about XCE behavior cites a line from external/.'

evidence.quote: Sonnet 4.0 + XCE (cascade hybrid): 76.8%
Every claim about XCE behavior in research.md cites a line from external/
suggests XCE supports a multi-pass strategy where context is fetched in stages — inference, not documented

verdict: SKIP — cascade hybrid closed-source; zero documentation beyond benchmark row (undocumented)

adoptItems: HLD/LLD template generation (RQ1), code_graph_trace (RQ2), code_graph_impact_analysis deterministic baseline (RQ3), PRAT Abstract stage (RQ5)
adaptItems: Combiner fold-in (RQ4), PRAT Persistent/Recursive/Tree stages (RQ5), Steering render.ts change (RQ6), Token measurement protocol (RQ8)
deferItems: Full PRAT pipeline closed-source (RQ5), Benchmark eval harness sub-packet 028 (RQ7)
